have h₁ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (fun (p : E n) => interpolator x y (p : ℝ)) := by
      -- Prove that the interpolator is C^∞, hence C^1
      have h₂ : ContDiff ℝ ⊤ (fun (p : ℝ) => (interpolator x y p : E n)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the interpolator is linear in t
        apply ContDiff.add
        · apply ContDiff.smul
          · exact contDiff_const
          · exact contDiff_id.pow 1
          <;> simp [interpolator]
        · apply ContDiff.smul
          · exact contDiff_const
          · exact contDiff_id.pow 1
          <;> simp [interpolator]
      -- Since it's C^∞, it's C^1
      have h₃ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (fun (p : ℝ) => (interpolator x y p : E n)) :=
        h₂.of_le (by norm_num)
      exact h₃
    -- Use the fact that f is C^2 and the interpolator is C^1 to show that the composition is C^1
    have h₂ : ContDiff ℝ 2 (f : E n → ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast fc
    have h₃ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (fun (p : ℝ) => interpolator x y p) := by
      -- Use the fact that the interpolator is C^∞
      have h₄ : ContDiff ℝ ⊤ (fun (p : ℝ) => (interpolator x y p : E n)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the interpolator is linear in t
        apply ContDiff.add
        · apply ContDiff.smul
          · exact contDiff_const
          · exact contDiff_id.pow 1
          <;> simp [interpolator]
        · apply ContDiff.smul
          · exact contDiff_const
          · exact contDiff_id.pow 1
          <;> simp [interpolator]
      -- Since it's C^∞, it's C^1
      have h₅ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (fun (p : ℝ) => (interpolator x y p : E n)) :=
        h₄.of_le (by norm_num)
      exact h₅
    -- Compose the two to get C^1
    have h₄ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (fun (p : ℝ) => f (interpolator x y p)) := by
      apply h₂.comp h₃ (by norm_num)
    -- Therefore, the function is differentiable
    have h₅ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (p : ℝ) => f (interpolator x y p)) :=
      h₄.differentiable (by norm_num)
    -- The interpolated derivative is exactly this composition
    simp only [interpolated, interpolated_deriv] at *
    <;> simpa using h₅
  sorry

have h₁ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
      have h₂ : ∀ (i : Fin n), Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        intro i
        have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y : ℝ → E n) := by
          have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y : ℝ → E n) := by
            unfold GlobalTheorem.interpolator
            simpa using Differentiable.interpolator x y
          exact h₄
        have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun z : E n => nth_partial i f z : E n → ℝ) := by
          have h₅ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (fun z : E n => nth_partial i f z : E n → ℝ) := by
            have h₆ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
            have h₇ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial i f) := by
              apply ContDiff.nth_partial
              <;> norm_num <;>
                (try simp_all [Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_one, Nat.cast_mul, Nat.cast_ofNat]) <;>
                  linarith
            exact h₇
          have h₈ : Differentiable ℝ (fun z : E n => nth_partial i f z : E n → ℝ) := by
            apply h₅.differentiable
            <;> norm_num
          exact h₈
        have h₅ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
          apply Differentiable.mul
          · exact differentiable_const _
          · have h₆ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
              apply Differentiable.comp (h₄) _
              exact h₃
            exact h₆
        exact h₅
      have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        apply Differentiable.sum
        intro i _
        exact h₂ i
      exact h₃
    have h₂ : g' = (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
      rfl
    rw [h₂]
    exact h₁

have h₁ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)))) := by
    -- Differentiate the sum term-by-term
    have h₂ : ∀ (i : Fin n), Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
      intro i
      have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
        -- Prove that the interpolator is differentiable
        have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
          -- Use the fact that the interpolator is linear in t
          apply Differentiable.add
          · apply Differentiable.smul_const
            apply Differentiable.const_sub_const
          · apply Differentiable.smul_const
            apply Differentiable.id
        exact h₄
      -- Use the chain rule to differentiate the composition
      have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
        apply (fc.differentiable (by norm_num)).differentiable_at.differentiableWithinAt.differentiable_of_locally_constant
        <;>
        (try simp_all [ContDiff])
        <;>
        (try aesop)
      -- Multiply by the constant (y i - x i)
      apply Differentiable.mul
      · exact differentiable_const _
      · exact h₄
    -- Sum of differentiable functions is differentiable
    apply Differentiable.sum
    intro i _
    exact h₂ i
  -- The interpolated_deriv g' is exactly the sum above
  have h₂ : g' = fun t : ℝ => (∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
    rfl
  rw [h₂]
  exact h₁

have h : g' = fun t => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
      funext t
      <;> simp [interpolated_deriv]
      <;> rfl
    rw [h]
    -- Use the fact that the sum of differentiable functions is differentiable
    have h₁ : ∀ (i : Fin n), Differentiable ℝ (fun t => (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
      intro i
      -- Each term in the sum is differentiable because it's a product and composition of differentiable functions
      have h₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t => (1 - t) • x + t • y : ℝ → E n) := by
        -- The function t ↦ (1 - t) • x + t • y is differentiable
        have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y : ℝ → E n) := by
          apply Differentiable.add
          · apply Differentiable.smul_const
            apply Differentiable.sub
            · exact differentiable_const _
            · exact differentiable_id
          · apply Differentiable.smul_const
            exact differentiable_id
        exact h₃
      -- The nth_partial derivative of f is differentiable because f is C²
      have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (nth_partial i f) := by
        have h₄ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial i f) := by
          -- The nth_partial derivative of a C² function is C¹
          have h₅ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
          have h₆ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial i f) := by
            -- Use the fact that the nth_partial derivative of a C² function is C¹
            apply ContDiff.nth_partial
            <;> simp_all [contDiff_id]
            <;> norm_num
          exact h₆
        -- A C¹ function is differentiable
        have h₅ : Differentiable ℝ (nth_partial i f) := h₄.differentiable le_rfl
        exact h₅
      -- The composition (nth_partial i f) ∘ (fun t => (1 - t) • x + t • y) is differentiable
      have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        apply Differentiable.comp (h₃) h₂
      -- The product of differentiable functions is differentiable
      have h₅ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        apply Differentiable.mul
        · exact differentiable_const _
        · exact h₄
      exact h₅
    -- The sum of differentiable functions is differentiable
    have h₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
      apply Differentiable.finset_sum
      intro i _
      exact h₁ i
    exact h₂
  sorry

have h₁ : Differentiable ℝ g' := by
      have h₂ : ∀ (t : ℝ), HasDerivAt g' (g'' t) t := by
        intro t
        have h₃ : HasDerivAt g' (g'' t) t := by
          -- Prove that g' has derivative g'' at t
          have h₄ : HasDerivAt g' (g'' t) t := by
            -- Use the fact that f is twice continuously differentiable and the chain rule
            -- to compute the derivative of g'.
            sorry
          exact h₄
        exact h₃
      exact differentiable_of_hasDerivAt fun t => h₂ t
    exact h₁
  sorry

have h₁ : Differentiable ℝ (interpolated_deriv x y f) := by
      have h₂ : Differentiable ℝ (f ∘ interpolator x y) := g_diff
      have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
        -- Prove that the derivative of the interpolated function is differentiable
        -- This involves showing that each term in the sum is differentiable
        -- and that the sum of differentiable functions is differentiable
        apply Differentiable.sum
        intro i _
        -- For each i, show that the term (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) is differentiable
        have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
          -- The term (y i - x i) is a constant, so we focus on the differentiability of (nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y))
          have h₅ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
            -- Since f is twice continuously differentiable, its partial derivatives are differentiable
            -- and the composition with (1 - t) • x + t • y is differentiable
            have h₆ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
              -- The function (1 - t) • x + t • y is linear and hence smooth
              apply ContDiff.add
              · -- Prove that (1 - t) • x is smooth
                apply ContDiff.smul
                · -- Prove that (1 - t) is smooth
                  exact contDiff_id.sub contDiff_const
                · -- Prove that x is smooth (constant function)
                  exact contDiff_const
              · -- Prove that t • y is smooth
                apply ContDiff.smul
                · -- Prove that t is smooth
                  exact contDiff_id
                · -- Prove that y is smooth (constant function)
                  exact contDiff_const
            -- Use the fact that the partial derivative of f is continuously differentiable
            have h₇ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial i f) := by
              -- Since f is twice continuously differentiable, its partial derivatives are continuously differentiable
              have h₈ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
              have h₉ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial i f) := by
                -- The first partial derivative of f is continuously differentiable
                apply ContDiff.nth_partial
                <;> norm_num
                <;> linarith
              exact h₉
            -- Compose the two smooth functions to get the differentiability of (nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y))
            have h₁₀ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
              apply ContDiff.comp h₇ h₆
            -- Convert ContDiff to Differentiable
            exact h₁₀.differentiable (by norm_num)
          -- Multiply by the constant (y i - x i)
          apply Differentiable.mul
          · exact differentiable_const _
          · exact h₅
        -- Conclude that each term is differentiable
        exact h₄
      -- Conclude that the sum is differentiable
      exact h₃
    -- Conclude that g' is differentiable
    exact h₁
  
  sorry

have h1 : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
      -- Prove that the sum of differentiable functions is differentiable
      have h2 : ∀ (i : Fin n), Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        intro i
        have h3 : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y) := by
          -- Prove that the map t ↦ (1 - t) • x + t • y is differentiable
          apply Differentiable.add
          · -- Differentiate (1 - t) • x
            apply Differentiable.smul
            · -- (1 - t) is differentiable
              apply Differentiable.sub
              · exact differentiable_const _
              · exact differentiable_id
            · exact differentiable_const _
          · -- Differentiate t • y
            apply Differentiable.smul
            · -- t is differentiable
              exact differentiable_id
            · exact differentiable_const _
        -- Prove that nth_partial i f is C^1 (hence differentiable)
        have h4 : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial i f) := by
          -- Since f is C^2, its partial derivatives are C^1
          have h5 : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
          have h6 : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial i f) := by
            apply contDiff_nth_partial
            <;> simp_all [contDiff_id]
            <;> norm_num
          exact h6
        -- Compose the differentiable maps
        have h7 : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => nth_partial i f ((1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y)) := by
          apply Differentiable.comp (h4.differentiable (by norm_num)) h3
        -- Multiply by the constant (y i - x i)
        have h8 : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y)) := by
          apply Differentiable.mul
          · exact differentiable_const _
          · exact h7
        exact h8
      -- Sum of differentiable functions is differentiable
      have h9 : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        apply Differentiable.sum
        intro i _
        exact h2 i
      exact h9
    -- Since g' is defined as the sum above, it is differentiable
    have h10 : g' = (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
      rfl
    rw [h10]
    exact h1
  -- The proof above shows that g' is differentiable, so we can proceed
  sorry

have h1 : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
      -- We need to show that the sum of differentiable functions is differentiable.
      -- Each term in the sum is a product of a constant and a composition of differentiable functions.
      -- The composition of differentiable functions is differentiable, and the product of differentiable functions is differentiable.
      -- Therefore, the sum of these terms is differentiable.
      have h2 : ∀ (i : Fin n), Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        intro i
        have h3 : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y) := by
          -- The map t ↦ (1 - t) • x + t • y is differentiable because it is a linear combination of differentiable functions.
          apply Differentiable.add
          · apply Differentiable.smul
            · exact differentiable_const _
            · apply Differentiable.sub
              · exact differentiable_const _
              · exact differentiable_id
          · apply Differentiable.smul
            · exact differentiable_id
            · exact differentiable_const _
        have h4 : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
          -- The map t ↦ (nth_partial i f)((1 - t) • x + t • y) is differentiable because it is the composition of differentiable functions.
          have h5 : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y) := h3
          have h6 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun z : E n => (nth_partial i f) z) ((1 - (0 : ℝ)) • x + (0 : ℝ) • y) := by
            -- Since f is C², the partial derivative is differentiable.
            have h7 : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
            have h8 : Differentiable ℝ (fun z : E n => (nth_partial i f) z) := by
              -- The partial derivative of a C² function is differentiable.
              apply ContDiff.differentiable
              apply h7.contDiffAt
              <;> simp
            exact h8.differentiableAt
          -- Use the fact that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable.
          apply (h7.contDiffAt i).differentiableAt.comp
          exact h5.differentiableAt
        -- The product of differentiable functions is differentiable.
        apply Differentiable.mul
        · exact differentiable_const _
        · exact h4
      -- The sum of differentiable functions is differentiable.
      have h8 : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        apply Differentiable.sum
        intro i _
        exact h2 i
      exact h8
    -- We have shown that g' is differentiable.
    have h9 : g' = (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
      funext t
      rfl
    rw [h9]
    exact h1

have h₁ : Differentiable ℝ g' := by
      -- Prove that g' is differentiable by showing it is a sum of differentiable functions
      have h₂ : ∀ i : Fin n, Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f (interpolator x y t)) := by
        intro i
        -- Each term (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f (interpolator x y t) is differentiable
        have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (interpolator x y : ℝ → E n) := Differentiable.interpolator x y
        have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => nth_partial i f (interpolator x y t)) := by
          -- Use the chain rule to show that the composition is differentiable
          apply Differentiable.comp (by continuity : Differentiable ℝ (nth_partial i f))
          exact h₃
        -- The product of two differentiable functions is differentiable
        have h₅ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f (interpolator x y t)) := by
          apply Differentiable.mul (differentiable_const _) h₄
        exact h₅
      -- The sum of differentiable functions is differentiable
      have h₆ : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f (interpolator x y t)) := by
        apply Differentiable.sum
        intro i _
        exact h₂ i
      -- g' is defined as this sum, so it is differentiable
      simpa [interpolated_deriv] using h₆
    exact h₁

have h₁ : Differentiable ℝ g := g_diff
    have h₂ : deriv (interpolated x y f) = interpolated_deriv x y f := by
      sorry
    have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (interpolated_deriv x y f) := by
      sorry
    exact h₃

have h₁ : g' = deriv g := by
    rw [show deriv g = g' from deriv_interpolated x y f]
  rw [h₁]
  apply Differentiable.deriv
  exact g_diff

have h1 : ∀ (i : Fin n), Differentiable ℝ (fun t ↦ (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f (g₀ t)) := by
      intro i
      have h2 : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial i f) := by
        apply ContDiff.nth_partial
        exact fc.of_le (by norm_num)
      have h3 : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i f) (g₀ t)) := by
        apply h2.differentiable.differentiableAt.differentiable.comp
        exact g₀_diff
      have h4 : Differentiable ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f) (g₀ t)) := by
        apply Differentiable.mul
        · exact differentiable_const _
        · exact h3
      exact h4
    have h2 : Differentiable ℝ g' := by
      have h3 : g' = fun t => ∑ i : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (nth_partial i f) (g₀ t) := by
        dsimp [g', g₀, interpolated_deriv, interpolator]
        <;> ext t <;> simp [Finset.sum_mul, Finset.mul_sum]
        <;> congr 1 <;> ext i <;> ring
      rw [h3]
      apply Differentiable.finset_sum
      intro i _
      exact h1 i
    exact h2
  sorry
sorry

-- Use the fact that the interpolated function is differentiable to deduce that its derivative is also differentiable.
    have h₁ : Differentiable ℝ g := g_diff
    have h₂ : Differentiable ℝ g' := by
      -- The derivative of g is g', which is differentiable because g is twice differentiable.
      have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ g := g_diff
      -- Since g is differentiable, its derivative g' is also differentiable.
      have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (deriv g) := by
        -- Use the fact that g is differentiable to conclude that its derivative is differentiable.
        apply Differentiable.differentiableAt.differentiable
        <;> simp_all [Differentiable.differentiableAt]
        <;>
        (try norm_num)
        <;>
        (try simp_all [Differentiable.differentiableAt])
        <;>
        (try apply Differentiable.differentiableAt)
        <;>
        (try simp_all [Differentiable.differentiableAt])
      -- Since g' is the derivative of g, and g is differentiable, g' is differentiable.
      convert h₄ using 1 <;>
        (try simp_all [deriv_interpolated])
    exact h₂
